description_long: This object is a classic portable boombox radio receiver with a rectangular black body and a prominent handle on top for easy carrying. The front face features two large circular speaker grilles, each with a radial pattern, and a central cassette tape slot with a yellow label. Above the speakers, there is a horizontal display panel with colored indicators and three round control knobs, likely for tuning, volume, and other adjustments. The sides and back are plain, with the back being completely featureless and the sides showing the depth and the protruding handle. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, typical of urban portable radios from the late 20th century.
description: A black, rectangular boombox radio receiver with dual front speakers, a cassette slot, control knobs, and a carrying handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large speaker grilles, a cassette slot, three control knobs, and a display panel with colored indicators.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the depth of the radio and the protruding handle, with no additional features visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain, featureless black surface with the handle visible on top.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the handle and the three control knobs protruding slightly.
